conventions.ca_bad: close(gamma) and no size >= n-r subset jointly explains (f1,f2); slope-free joint clause, so CA-bad = close set for pair-far pairs, empty for pair-close pairs
conventions.extras: MCA-bad \ CA-bad (the mutual-only / sparse layer, via the thm:sparsify translation bijection)
conventions.field_ledger: toy prime rows use q_gen=q_line; q_chal=null; no soundness division claimed
conventions.mca_bad: close(gamma) and some FULL agreement set of a within-r codeword of f1+gamma*f2 is unshared (f1 or f2 does not restrict to deg<k); equals the mca_frontier engine's same_set_failures; equivalent to the sigma-c-sparse-census maximal S_z eps2-only check by lem:line's final equivalence
conventions.radius_convention: closed radius r = floor(delta*n); agreement A = n-r
conventions.symmetry_policy: no symmetry quotient; no Mobius reduction; no point at infinity
conventions.tangents: { -e1(j)/e2(j) : e2(j) != 0 }, e_i = f_i - p_i, per pair in L(r)
conventions.violations: extras outside the tangent union of the exact radius-r interleaved pair list L(r)

exact_equality_window.statement: at F_97 n=16 k=8 r=5 (the integer Johnson / GS-complete radius, J = 0.0046) extras == tangent union of the radius-r pair list EXACTLY, zero exceptions

j_density_law.heuristic_flood_mass_per_instance: q*J ~ C(n,r) * q^(1+r-(n-k)); decays in q for r <= n-k-2
j_density_law.statement: J(n,k,q,r) = sum_{s>=n-r} C(n,s)(q-1)^(n-s)/q^(n-k) (ball volume x code density) tracks violation density across all censused cells; the wall is at list-decoding CAPACITY (r -> n-k, J ~ 1), not at the Johnson radius

killer_counterexample.control_r2: same pair at the unique radius r=2 is pair-far; extras empty
killer_counterexample.statement: the tangent-containment extension of thm:mca-from-ca is FALSE one step past the unique radius; |extras| <= |L(r)|*r is dead (17 > 3)

rplus1_repair_fails.statement: extending the pair list to radius r+1 (with >= D-r simultaneous-cancellation tangency) repairs A r=3 and B r=5 but FAILS at B r=4: all 5 violating slopes have witnesses only at depth n-k = r+2, so the repair radius must reach n-k, not r+1.

headline.sharpest_supported_statement: for every instance and radius censused: every MCA-bad slope outside CA-bad either lies in the tangent union of the exact radius-r pair list L(r), or carries only witnesses that decompose at joint distance exactly n-k with >= D-r simultaneous cancellations at the slope; m = n-k is sufficient in every censused cell and necessary (B r=4); the deep clause has empirical density <= J per close slope, exactly zero in the Johnson-edge window J << 1

F97_n16_k8:r6.note: targeted 28-challenge sample; depths via exact T-restricted interpolation

witness_depth_invariant.statement: every violating witness decomposes (q = a + z*b, minimized over all q^k decompositions; exact T-restricted interpolation for row C) only at joint distance exactly n-k: no structured explanation shallower than the generic interpolation floor

non_claims: No statement for general (n,k,q); no bound past the censused radii.
No deployed-row statement; all oracles exact but exponential.
The J-law is a measured correlation across 8 cells, not a proved bound.
Row C r=6 densities are estimates from one targeted 28-challenge sample.
No GPU search result; no q_chal soundness division.

positioning.related_pr: PR #272 (conditional BCHKS25 Thm 4.6 / Hab25 import) cites this census as its toy statement-shape cross-check
positioning.vs_sigma_c_sparse_census: the sigma-c lane records sigma_C VALUES per row (saturation sigma_C=q_line via extremal S_z witnesses; sigma_C=r in the trivial regime); this packet characterizes the STRUCTURE of the layer (containment shape, witness depth, equality window, J wall). sigma_C is a max over all sparse pairs; our densities are family averages -- the extremal pair can saturate at modest J. toy saturation does not falsify thm:transfer's poly-sigma_C hypothesis because q_line ~ n at toy scale

C.oracle: engine-driven; GS exact at r=5; r=6 = exact C(16,8) subset enumeration, ONE targeted 28-challenge instance

oracle_budget.row_c_r5: engine Guruswami-Sudan list decoding (exact at the GS-complete radius 5)
oracle_budget.row_c_r6: engine exact C(16,8) = 12870-subset enumeration per challenge; one targeted 28-challenge instance only (full r=6 census out of budget)
oracle_budget.rows_ab: exact enumeration of all q^k codewords (17^4 = 83521 / 17^2 = 289) + C(8, 8-r) subset pair lists; exponential in k, exact

status: AUDIT/EXPERIMENTAL: compact extract of an externally frozen toy census; computational evidence, not a theorem
